_meta.author: Claude (national autopilot batch)
_meta.note: GA legislature batch (state tier): SD-11 Watson, HD-12 Lumsden, SD-12 Sims (D), SD-13 Summers, HD-13 Dempsey. Evidence from The Freedom Index (individual recorded GA roll calls), GA Life Alliance pro-life certification, NRA-PVF / Family Policy Alliance endorsements, iVoterGuide questionnaires, bill sponsorships, committee/career records, and strict party-line marquee-bill reporting (HB481, SB140, SB233, SB202, HB1105, SB319). DEVIATION: Watson (R) voted AGAINST SB233 school vouchers (rural-R). Sims (D) is a consistent Democratic-bloc progressive (documented FALSEs) and ANNOUNCED 2026-03 she will NOT seek re-election (candidacy_status fix). GA individual roll-call PDFs were not machine-fetchable this session, so unconfirmed individual votes are left null; only documented votes/ratings/self-statements scored.

profile.religion: Baptist — member and Deacon, Moultrie First Baptist Church
profile.background: GA Senate District 11 (SW Georgia: Brooks, Colquitt, Cook, Decatur, Grady, Seminole, Thomas) since a Feb 2023 special election; prior GA House HD-172 (2013-2022). Agricultural producer/farmer; past President, Georgia Fruit & Vegetable Growers Assn. Chairs Senate Ethics.

sam-watson@GA.notes_set: GA State Senator, SD-11 (R), since 2023 (prior Delegate 2013-2022). [2026-06 national refinement, state tier] Conservative with a notable rural-R deviation. Per The Freedom Index recorded votes: FOR SB140 (2023 ban on gender-transition care for minors); FOR HB1178 Parents' Bill of Rights; FOR HB1084 Protect Students First Act (anti-'divisive concepts'/CRT); AGAINST HB206 'Georgia Green New Deal'; FOR SB67 Second Amendment Preservation Act (anti-commandeering) + vaccine-passport ban (anti-mandate); FOR HB1105 sanctuary-city ban; FOR SB319 constitutional carry, SB572 self-defense civil-immunity, and HB1018 firearm-merchant-code ban (anti-surveillance). DEVIATION: voted AGAINST SB233 (2024 Georgia Promise Scholarship vouchers) — rural-Republican opposition; school_choice FALSE. His Freedom Index score has declined (2021-22 ~80% -> 2025-26 ~45%). Life-issue cells left null (HB481 individual vote unconfirmed; no GLA rating; HB428 IVF vote ambiguous).

2.note: Voted FOR SB140 (2023) banning hormone therapy/surgery (gender transition) for minors.

0.note: DEVIATION: voted AGAINST SB233 (2024 Georgia Promise Scholarship vouchers/school choice) — rural-Republican opposition (passed Senate 33-21).

1.note: Voted FOR HB1178 Parents' Bill of Rights (parental access/consent on minors' education and records).

2.note: Voted FOR HB1084 Protect Students First Act (GA anti-'divisive concepts'/CRT-in-classrooms law).

4.note: Voted AGAINST HB206 'Georgia Green New Deal' green-financing mechanism — anti-ESG/green-mandate posture.

0.note: Voted FOR SB67 Second Amendment Preservation Act (anti-commandeering / firearms-sovereignty).

1.note: Voted FOR the vaccine-passport ban / anti-mandate cluster (declined federal-style mandates).

2.note: SB67 firearms-preservation act resists federal overreach on guns.
Voted FOR HB1105 (2024) sanctuary-city penalty / mandatory cooperation with federal immigration enforcement.

3.note: Voted FOR SB572 (2026) self-defense / civil-immunity expansion (stand-your-ground / castle-doctrine direction).

4.note: Voted FOR HB1018 (2024) firearm-merchant-code prohibition (anti-financial-surveillance of gun buyers).

profile.religion: Southern Baptist — Deacon and worship leader, Pleasant Valley North Baptist Church (Floyd County); serves on the church security team
profile.background: GA House District 12 (Chattooga Co. + part of Floyd Co./Rome-Armuchee) since 2013. Air Force/Air Guard veteran; retired master trooper after 27 years in the Georgia State Patrol; State Farm insurance agency co-owner; former Floyd County Commissioner. Chairs the House Insurance Committee.

eddie-lumsden@GA.notes_set: GA State Representative, HD-12 (R), since 2013. [2026-06 national refinement, state tier] Thinly documented at the member-vote level — GA individual roll-call PDFs were not machine-fetchable this session, so party-line bill characterizations were NOT scored. Scored only on documented, defensible facts: a 27-year Georgia State Patrol career + sponsorship of HB238 (POST at-risk-adult/dementia officer-training, signed into law) + Public Safety & Homeland Security committee seat = affirmative public-justice/pro-law-enforcement record; and a self-stated position supporting voluntary prayer in public schools (candidate survey). Won the 2026-05-19 GOP primary; ran unopposed in 2024. No cross-party deviation found. All other dimensions left null pending cited votes.

3.note: Candidate survey: supports voluntary prayer in public schools.

0.note: Retired 27-year Georgia State Patrol master trooper; sits on Public Safety & Homeland Security; sponsored HB238 (POST officer-training, signed) — affirmative, sustained backing of law enforcement.

profile.religion: Baptist (devout, per official GA Senate bio)
profile.background: GA Senate District 12 (SW Georgia: Albany/Dougherty area) since 2009; GA House 2005-2009. Retired middle-school principal; Ph.D. in Educational Leadership; former Dougherty County deputy registrar.

freddie-sims@GA.notes_set: GA State Senator, SD-12 (D), since 2009. [2026-06 national refinement, state tier] CANDIDACY CURRENCY: announced 2026-03 she will NOT seek a tenth term (husband's illness) — still holds the seat through Jan 2027 (status active) but candidacy_status set not_running for the 2026 cycle. Consistent Democratic-bloc voting record on marquee bills (documented FALSEs, cited to strict party-line reporting): opposed HB481 heartbeat act (sanctity-of-life), SB140 minors' gender-care ban (transgender), SB233 vouchers (school choice), SB202 election-integrity/voter-ID act, HB1105 sanctuary-city ban, and SB319 constitutional carry; co-signed an official 2025 statement condemning anti-DEI legislation (HB127). SB63 cash-bail individual vote left null (17 NO votes < full Dem caucus; not individually confirmed). No cross-party deviation found.

0.note: In the Democratic bloc opposing HB481 (2019 LIFE Act / heartbeat bill); Senate passed 34-18, present Democrats all voted NO.

2.note: In the Democratic bloc opposing SB140 (2023) ban on gender-transition care for minors (party-line 31-21).

0.note: In the Democratic bloc opposing SB233 (2024 Georgia Promise Scholarship vouchers/school choice); passed 33-21.

2.note: Co-signed an official 2025 Senate statement condemning anti-DEI legislation (HB127) — supports DEI in schools/universities, contrary to opposing CRT/SOGI.

1.note: In the Democratic bloc opposing SB202 (2021 Election Integrity Act, incl. voter-ID provisions); party-line Republican measure Democrats opposed.

2.note: In the Democratic bloc opposing HB1105 (2024) sanctuary-city ban; Senate passed 34-19, minority Democrats all voted against.

0.note: In the Democratic bloc opposing SB319 (2022) constitutional/permitless carry (party-line 34-22); also co-sponsored a safe-firearm-storage measure.

profile.religion: Christian (lifelong churchgoer; no specific denomination/congregation documented)
profile.background: GA Senate District 13 (central/south GA: Cordele/Crisp + Ben Hill, Berrien, Irwin, Lee, Tift, Turner, Worth, part of Coffee) since a March 2020 special election. 40-year businessman (farmer, real-estate broker); former Crisp County Commissioner. Chairs Senate Banking & Financial Institutions.

carden-summers@GA.notes_set: GA State Senator, SD-13 (R), since 2020. [2026-06 national refinement, state tier] Marquee social-conservative sponsor. PRIME SPONSOR of SB88 'Parents and Children Protection Act of 2023' (the 'Don't Say Gay' parental-consent-on-gender-instruction bill, public + private schools); co-sponsor SB140 (minors' gender-care ban); co-sponsor SB390 (defund ALA-affiliated libraries) and sponsor SB74 (criminal penalties for librarians providing harmful/obscene material to minors); listed sponsor of SB319 constitutional carry. Self-stated (iVoterGuide): man-woman marriage, border wall + opposes funding sanctuary cities. Note a soft 2A nuance (stated support for a 24-hr handgun waiting period for non-permit-holders) and NOT on the 2024 GA Life Alliance certification list. Sanctity/elections/justice individual votes left null (unconfirmed).

0.note: Self-stated: governments should define marriage as between one man and one woman.

2.note: Prime sponsor of SB88 (parental-consent on gender-identity instruction) and co-sponsor of SB140 (minors' gender-care ban) — rejects transgender ideology in schools.

1.note: SB88 requires parental permission before instruction on gender identity and before changing a student's name/sex/gender in school records.

2.note: SB88 bars instruction on gender identity/'queer theory'/gender transition to minors without parental consent.

3.note: Sponsor of SB74 creating criminal penalties for librarians who provide obscene/harmful material to minors; co-sponsor SB390 (defund ALA-affiliated libraries).

2.note: Self-stated: opposes funding for sanctuary cities.

0.note: Listed among sponsors of SB319, the Georgia Constitutional Carry Act (permitless carry). Note: separately stated support for a 24-hr handgun waiting period for non-permit-holders (soft control nuance).

profile.religion: Christian — member/trustee, Rome First United Methodist Church
profile.background: GA House District 13 (Rome / parts of Floyd Co.) since 2007. UGA engineering graduate; former Rome City Commissioner; senior appropriations figure (chairs Appropriations Subcommittee on Human Resources; on the Committee on Assignments). Known for health/behavioral-health policy.

katie-dempsey@GA.notes_set: GA State Representative, HD-13 (R), since 2007. [2026-06 national refinement, state tier] Reliable on the documented social-conservative markers despite a 'moderate'/bipartisan health-appropriations reputation (ACU GA ~52% in 2019 rising to ~82% in 2023). Scored on hard credentials and clear self-statements: GA Life Alliance pro-life certified + iVoterGuide 'life begins at conception'; spoke in committee FOR SB140 minors' gender-care restriction; supports voter ID (opposes voting without photo ID); iVoterGuide border wall + opposes sanctuary cities; NRA-PVF endorsed + self-states passing constitutional carry. SB233 voucher / SB63 bail / HB1105 individual votes left null (unconfirmed; 16 House R's voted NO on SB233 — could not rule her in or out). No documented anti-conservative roll call found.

0.note: GA Life Alliance pro-life certified candidate; iVoterGuide 'Strongly Agree' life begins at conception and deserves legal protection.

2.note: Spoke in support of SB140 minors' gender-care restriction ('the brain is not fully developed till age 25') — affirms restricting minors' transition decisions.

1.note: iVoterGuide: 'Strongly Disagree' that people should vote without photo ID (supports voter ID); self-claims passing SB202 Election Integrity Act.

0.note: iVoterGuide: 'Strongly Agree' a border wall is needed.
Self-states passing 'Constitutional Carry' (SB319, 2022) permitless carry; supports armed teachers.

1.note: NRA-PVF endorsed — hard evidence of opposition to gun-control measures (red-flag/AWB/registries).
